title: Multimine
description: Adds an enchantment that allows pickaxes and shovels to mine multiple blocks at the same time.
body:
# Usage
This datapack adds a new enchantment - Multimine.

![An enchanted pickaxe with the "Multimine" enchantment, at level 5.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/69cacd6d316965aa5962fcb144979e3b6d262be1.png)

To obtain Multimine, simply enchant a pickaxe or shovel with Multimine using an enchanting table or anvil. To use Multimine, simply break any block. The enchantment will break the blocks around in that are **common tool blocks**; a common tool block is a block that appears commonly in the world and is relatively easy to mine with the tool.

The full list of common pickaxe blocks:

- Stone
- Infested Stone
- Granite
- Diorite
- Andesite
- Tuff
- All 3 kinds of cobblestone
- All 3 kinds of deepslate
- Dripstone Block
- Calcite
- Both kinds of sandstone
- All 17 kinds of terracotta
- Netherrack
- Basalt
- Blackstone
- Magma Block
- Both kinds of nylium
- End stone

Note that ores are not included.

The full list of common shovel blocks:

- All 3 kinds of dirt
- Grass Block
- Podzol
- Mycelium
- Both kinds of moss block *(note that these are actually easier to mine with a hoe)*
- Mud
- Muddy Mangrove Roots
- All 3 kinds of sand
- Both kinds of gravel
- Clay
- All 3 kinds of snow

There are 5 levels of Multimine, which each allow you to break more common tool blocks:

1. Allows you to break the block underneath the block you mined
 ![Visualization of the effect of Multimine I, with a barrier as the block mined by the user, and glass as the blocks broken by Multimine.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/9c4a9844daf11f43bee997a125e84cd397e68cba.png)
2. Allows you to break the blocks face adjacent to the block you mined
 ![Visualization of the effect of Multimine II, with a barrier as the block mined by the user, and glass as the blocks broken by Multimine.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/8d5f385ce396dccd6faaebf8c6c076d48f12c59f.png)
3. Allows you to break the blocks face or edge adjacent to the block you mined
 ![Visualization of the effect of Multimine III, with a barrier as the block mined by the user, and glass as the blocks broken by Multimine.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/581a46b1c2262312cea3e048565e2c0a4235a4c9.png)
4. Allows you to break the blocks face or edge or vertex adjacent to the block you mined
 ![Visualization of the effect of Multimine IV, with a barrier as the block mined by the user, and glass as the blocks broken by Multimine.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/2893364e4a031cf688e66eab04abd2b84b070166.png)
5. Allows you to break the blocks face or edge or vertex adjacent to the block you mined, the blocks vertically adjacent to those blocks, or the blocks face adjacent to those
 ![Visualization of the effect of Multimine V, with a barrier as the block mined by the user, and glass as the blocks broken by Multimine.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/8e0db6e1cefc319fef7af85588527a4951b2309a.png)

Multimine works with other enchantments such as Fortune or Silk Touch.
Multimine currently has a bug where instamine does not work with it.

# Installation
## Datapack
### On Create World
1. Click the *More* tab
2. Click *Data Packs*
3. Click *Open Pack Folder*
4. Paste in the downloaded datapack
5. Move the datapack to *Selected*
6. Click *Done*
### Add to existing world
1. Select the world
2. Click *Edit*
3. Click *Open World Folder*
4. Open the `datapacks` folder
5. Paste in the downloaded datapack
## Required Resource Pack
### Singleplayer
1. Open the resource pack list
2. Click *Open Pack Folder*
3. Paste in the downloaded resource pack
4. Move the resource pack to *Selected*
### Server
1. Add the downloaded resource pack as a server resource pack
2. Make server resource packs required
# Removal
1. Do `/function multimine:remove` to remove all the scoreboards.
2. Remove the datapack

# Compatibility
This datapack is not currently intentionally compatible with other datapacks or mods

However, it should be compatible with most mods or datapacks that don't change how pickaxes or shovels work, and don't add new common tool blocks.
